result.summary: The page is mostly functional but shows several layout clipping issues, primarily content cut off at the right edge of the viewport and at the bottom where the navigation bar overlaps game tiles. There are also some minor contrast and truncation concerns.

issues.description: Game tiles in the third column are cut off at the right edge of the viewport in both 'BetRivers Exclusive Games' and 'Top Played New Games' sections (e.g., 'MAGNI...PHO...', 'MUNC...MI...', 'HYPERC...'), making titles unreadable.
issues.suggestion: Ensure horizontal carousels show partial tiles intentionally with a visible scroll affordance, or adjust tile sizing/spacing so tiles are not awkwardly clipped mid-content.

issues.description: The last navigation pill in the top category bar is truncated to 'Hot Gar' (likely 'Hot Games'), with the label cut off at the screen edge.
issues.suggestion: Add horizontal scroll indicators or a fade/gradient edge to signal more categories, or size pills so labels aren't visibly clipped mid-word.

issues.description: The bottom row of game thumbnails ('Who Wants to Be a Millionaire', 'Rakin Bacon', 'Dark Waters') is partially hidden behind the fixed bottom navigation bar, cutting off game artwork and titles.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding to the scrollable content area so the last row of tiles clears the fixed bottom navigation.

issues.description: The left carousel arrow on the yellow promo banner is a pale/white chevron on a light yellow background with very low contrast, making it hard to see.
issues.suggestion: Increase the arrow's contrast, e.g., use a dark chevron or add a semi-transparent dark circular background behind it.

issues.description: The 'Casino' item in the bottom navigation appears underlined and styled differently (serif/underlined link) from the other uppercase nav labels, creating visual inconsistency.
issues.suggestion: Apply a consistent active-state style (e.g., color highlight) matching the typography of the other bottom nav items instead of an underlined text link.

issues.description: The small 'i' (info) buttons on game tiles are low-contrast dark chips over dark artwork and are very small tap targets on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Increase the tap target size to at least 44x44px and improve contrast of the info icon against tile backgrounds.
